01. Scope of Engagement & ASM Approval.d:
01. Introduction and Scope of Engagement

We, M/s Dharmraj & Co., Chartered Accountants, have been appointed as statutory auditors of ABC Limited under Section 139 of the Companies Act, 2013 (CA 2013) by the members in the Annual General Meeting held on [AGM Date]. Our appointment is valid until the conclusion of the [AGM Year]. This is [first/second/third] year of our audit engagement of this company.

The Company operates primarily in [industry segment, e.g. passenger cars and commercial vehicles], with a manufacturing base in [India / European Country] and export footprints in other overseas jurisdictions. ABC Limited has [number] manufacturing plants in India at [City 1] and [City 2]. ABC Limited does not use any service organisation.

ABC Limited's equity shares are listed on [BSE / NSE / both]. The engagement covers the audit of the Standalone Financial Statements (SFS) of the company for the year ended 31.03.20XY.

Additional Reporting Responsibilities

Under CA 2013

Matters specified under Section 143(3) including CARO 2020, adequacy of internal financial controls (ICFR), audit trail, and other prescribed matters.

Fraud reporting under Section 143(12) of CA 2013.

SEBI Listing Regulations

Limited review of quarterly financial results as per SEBI Listing Regulations.

02. Planning Meeting with Client & Engag:
02. Planning Meetings with Client and Engagement Team

I. Planning Meeting with Engagement Team

Date of meeting: [Insert date]

Participants

Engagement Partner — CA. Dharmaraj

EQCR — CA. Dronacharya

Team members — CA. Bhim, CA. Arjun, CA. Abhimanyu & CA. Ghatotkacha

Auditor's Experts — CA. Nakul, IS Audit Expert

Key Matters Discussed

1. Sharing Knowledge of the Entity

CA. Bhim, Engagement Manager (EM1), shared understanding of the entity including organizational structure, ownership and governance, business model, industry, economic environment, operations and key staff.

CA. Arjun, Engagement Manager (EM2), informed the team about information obtained during the acceptance and continuance process.

CA. Dharmaraj, Engagement Partner (EP) gave overview of Scope and Reporting Responsibilities.

2. Collective Responsibility for Audit Quality

CA. Dharmaraj, EP apprised the ET of firm's Quality Control Policy Manual and Audit Methodology Manual — including open communication policies and raising concerns without fear of reprisal.

3. Professional Ethics and Independence

CA. Arjun, EM2 highlighted compliance with Firm's Code of Conduct, independence rules, confidentiality, and NOCLAR responsibilities.

4. Professional Skepticism

CA. Dharmaraj and CA. Dronacharya emphasised maintaining a questioning mind, awareness of unconscious biases, and how/why fraud may occur.

5. Materiality

CA. Sahdev, Engagement Supervisor briefed on Overall Materiality, Performance Materiality, Clearly Trivial Threshold, and Specific/Component Materiality.

6. Team Responsibilities

Engagement Partner: Overall direction and review.

EQCR: Objective evaluation of significant judgments.

Managers: Supervision and liaison with client.

Experts: [IT System Testing / Actuarial Valuation / Fair Value]

7. Timelines

Interim audit: [Insert start–end dates]

Year-end audit: [Insert start–end dates]

II. Planning Meeting with Client Management

Date of meeting: [Insert Date]

Participants

From Auditor's side: Engagement Partner, Manager, Supervisor

From Client's side: [CFO, Head of Finance, Internal Audit Head, Company Secretary]

Key Matters Discussed

1. Business Environment Update

[Describe significant changes in operations during the financial year, including expansion, new products, regulatory developments, their financial impact]

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms — auditors to consider adherence when evaluating contingent liabilities, provisions, and disclosures under [applicable Ind AS, e.g. Ind AS 37]

2. Regulatory / Subsidiary Matters

[Insert details of any regulatory inspections, e.g. RBI inspection of NBFC subsidiary. Cross-reference Section VII.]

3. IT System Updates

[Describe significant ERP/IT changes during the year — migration, automation, new interfaces]

4. Financial Performance

[Summarise standalone and consolidated financial highlights — key changes in revenue, costs, provisions]

5. Actions Agreed

Time plans for interim audit; client to advise draft FS and committee meeting dates; regular status meetings weekly during fieldwork.

03. Overview of Business and Industry Environment

This section documents industry conditions, volatility, competitive dynamics, technological and regulatory developments relevant to ABC Limited ('Company').

Market / Industry

The Company operates primarily in [industry segments]. The industry is [capital-intensive / labour-intensive / technology-driven], dependent on [key supply chain inputs]. ABC Limited has [number] subsidiaries in India and Overseas.

Significant Market Changes / Volatility

[Describe revenue trend over last 3 years and key pressures: raw material inflation, geopolitical risks, policy shifts etc.]

Macro-economic Conditions

[Describe correlation with GDP, interest rates, credit availability, and their impact on the client's business]

Competition

The [industry] sector is fiercely competitive. ABC Limited competes with [competitor names].

Technological Developments

[Describe key technology trends impacting the business — EV transition, digitalisation, automation, R&D]

Peer Comparison

[Compare margins, revenue growth, export revenue vs peers]

Market Position

[Describe market share, positioning, brand strength vs peers]

Compliance with Laws and Regulations

Key applicable laws include:

• SEBI Listing Regulations — Related Party Disclosures, timely event disclosures

• Income-tax Act, 1961 — corporate taxation and transfer pricing

• GST — applicable rates on [product categories]

• Environment Protection Act, Extended Producer Responsibility Rules

• Information Technology Act, Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023

[Add any other sector-specific regulations applicable to this client]

Other Industry-Specific Risks

[Supply chain, forex exposure, ESG/climate risks — describe specific exposures of this client with amounts where known]

Communication with TCWG and Audit Committee

CA. Dharmaraj, Engagement Partner advised the CFO about requirements of SA 260 (Revised) and SA 265. Two-way communication protocols to be established with the Board and Audit Committee.

08. Overview of Internal Control Systems:
08. Overview of Internal Control Systems Including IT Platforms

(This section documents understanding of internal control systems to determine control risk and audit strategy.)

This is our [first/second/third] year audit of ABC Limited. Based on our discussion with CFO and Head of Internal Audit, we will be [placing / not placing] reliance on prior year documentation.

Internal Control Environment

[Describe the key elements of the client's internal control environment: policies & procedures manual, budget/MIS, Internal Audit function, Risk Management function, Audit Committee composition]

Information Technology Platforms

[List key IT systems used by the client — ERP for GL, HR/payroll system, asset management, sales/inventory systems, and any automated interfaces]

During the year, ABC Limited has [describe significant IT changes / upgrades / migrations]. Our IS Engagement Team will perform a detailed review of IT platforms, data migration, and automated controls.

Risk of Material Misstatement (ROMM) Assessment Matrix

We will assess FSLI-wise preliminary ROMM using the firm's standard 3×3 Inherent Risk × Control Risk matrix.

[Engagement teams will elaborate this based on facts and circumstances during the detailed audit planning phase.]
09. Significant Accounting & Auditing Ma:
09. Significant Accounting and Auditing Matters

Based on review of financial performance, material information disclosed to stock exchanges, and preliminary discussions with management, we have noted the following areas of significance.

Standalone Financial Statements

Employee Benefits

[Describe any significant employee benefit events — e.g. surrender/devolvement of EPF trust, actuarial obligations. Note the need for external actuarial expert.]

Business Restructuring

[Describe any restructuring, demerger, or disposal transactions during/after the year — regulatory approvals pending, accounting implications, assets/liabilities to be derecognised]

Consolidated Financial Statements

Intangible Assets Under Development

[Describe the significant amounts, Ind AS 36/38 implications, overseas subsidiary involvement, liaison with component auditors]

Business Combination

[Describe any planned acquisition — method of accounting (Ind AS 103), need for fair value expert, expected timing]

Warranty Provisions

[Describe significant increase in warranty provisions — reason (extended warranties, change in accrual method), involvement of actuaries/experts]

Expected Credit Loss (ECL) for Subsidiary Loans

[Describe credit quality deterioration in loan portfolio, regulator inspection findings, working with subsidiary auditor on ECL under Ind AS 109]

Significant Areas Due to Inherent Industry Risk

• [Revenue recognition] — multiple channels, bundled arrangements, SA 115 considerations

• [Inventory valuation] — costing methodology, overhead absorption, obsolescence

• [Impairment of PPE / intangibles / goodwill] — cash flow forecasts, discount rates

• [ESG/Environmental obligations] — EPR norms, climate commitments, provisions & contingencies

New Accounting Developments

[List relevant Ind AS amendments notified by MCA and NFRA circulars applicable to this client for the current year]

New Auditing Developments

[Note relevant SA revisions or NFRA clarificatory circulars applicable — e.g. SA 315, group audit circular, fraud reporting circular]
